profile_bio.description: I enjoy drinking good wine as much as I do trading stocks; they also happen to both cost me money.

quoted_tweet.text:
🐋 $SOFI Dark Pool — 3-Day

59.8M shares just landed at $15. The zone that sat at $16–$18 two weeks ago has collapsed back to the level that held in early April.

🥇 $15 → 59.8M shares (heavily DP)
🥈 $18 → 31.8M shares (green dominant)
🥉 $16 → 26.8M shares (green dominant)

$15 has appeared as the dominant anchor twice now as institutions keep coming back to the same level. 

Price is sitting just above it with the entire prior zone of $16–$18 now sitting overhead as resistance. Last time $15 held, the zone rebuilt all the way to $18.73.

Smart money has been here before. The question is whether they show up again — or whether this time they're the ones selling into it.

kept.text:
Why choose $SOFI instead of a traditional bank?👇

SoFi has no physical branches, so it can pass the savings on to customers.

SoFi offers:
• High-yield banking with no fees
• A true all-in-one financial app (checking, savings, investing, loans, credit card, and personal finance tools in one interface)
• No hidden fees that traditional banks still charge (maintenance, overdraft, transfer fees, etc.)
• Faster, cleaner digital experience built mobile-first instead of branch-first
• Integrated investing alongside banking (not a separate system or app)
• Real-time financial tracking (credit score, spending insights, net worth in one dashboard)
• Faster onboarding and simpler account setup without paperwork or branch visits

Traditional banks feel like separate products stitched together. 

SoFi feels like a unified financial operating system with benefits ⚡️

kept.text:
Holy sh*t

$SoFi Exciting announcements to come shortly https://t.co/VloDtpBapi

$sofi

I'm reposting this as additional information to consider. 

The ramp up of big business banking and starting with international payments especially in South America as I described in detail in my Trojan Horse series does relate to sofi shifting from loan sales to holding loans last quarter. 

The reason you should hold loans is you get a higher return and it's spread out over what Noto said was 3 years.  I also expect them to continue that focus in the second quarter until they have enough yield on the loans held on the balance sheet to at least significantly offset expenses related to starting big business banking.

Typically you will incur substantial expenses without significant return on establishing this new business for 12 to 18 months. Which is one of the reasons after 4th quarter earnings I said that eps in 1st quarter probably would be lower and I predicted a brutal decline in stock price after earnings. 

Markets don't understand what sofi is doing,  but I do. 

What they are doing in the interim is maximizing the return on those loans over the next 18 months to 3 years to normalize eps growth and not risk a decline in a period of big investments. 

Sure, if you sold those loans, you'd have no risk but they are keeping the highest quality loans. They still will have good lpb sales, but you need that shift (which is why the capital raises were necessary and very cleverly strategic).

You don't want to have a no rate cut environment or a possible raise environment in a period of large investments and not have existing return because you could have an increase in loan platform sales in one quarter and then a down quarter (coupled with increased expenses) and you would not want the large fluctuations in your eps to not meet expectations (or worse fall substantially quarter to quarter).

THIS IS A VERY SMART MOVE. NOTO AND TEAM KNOW WHAT THEY ARE DOING

THE TROJAN HORSE SERIES IS WHAT INVESTORS SHOULD BE FOCUSING ON

(And a little tidbit... my write up below may need to be revised because I think there is a way that Sofi can offer yield to big businesses and members on stablecoins held in their system) you'll have to wait later on why that is huge just like you'll have to wait for more info on MB)

quoted_tweet.text:
$sofi

THE SOFI STABLECOIN FUTURE

Who would have thought about using your invest/margin account to secure a cash loan.... oh yes I mentioned that for 2 years 

Here's another tidbit.

B³ vs MB,  I think we can expect that stablecoins will be more integrated with Sofi Plus or a premium Sofi Plus and a premium credit card. 

Think of it like this... if you have a stablecoin balance of $5000 or $10,000.... then you qualify for enhanced Sofi premium credit card and other benefits...

While you won't get interest on your stablecoin balance, because it's prohibited under the Clarity act, you will likely get an enhanced credit card.. and other benefits that will more than offset the lack of interest likely resulting in a 10%+ return, which could be even after paying say $20 per month for the card.

Now also consider. If you have 30 million members and 10 million having $5,000 balances in Sofi USD... that's $50 billion of Sofi USD, then expand that stablecoin use to a checking account and bill pay with stablecoin and mix that with business banking to secure those payments and you can see how Sofi can ramp that and you then are able to peer over B³ to the 2028 future of MB

$SOFI CEO: “We didn’t raise the guidance because we don’t expect any rate cuts this year.”

This is a great time to enter $SOFI at a discount.

They are always more conservative than the consensus, so they tend to surprise on the upside if macro conditions end up being better than they expected.

We get a single cut this year and $SOFI will go brrr..

The upside vastly outstrips the downside here.

Long $SOFI.
